post.author: Andreas Leijonmarck
post.title: Strategies
post.body: Mar 24  • General 💭Strategies1 great video with the proper hooks, 1 great offer in the end of it(do you show your offer before a sales call?). 1 epic video ad - ending with a brief notion of 1 epic offer - and a summoning to "see if you are qualified for a call".Thoughts on this thought process ? As contrary to grinding cold paths. I want results, and efficiency. I already have the capacity to back up the clients with high quality work once their in. I just need to open the door for them to enter more efficiently.Whilst mov... See more

post.author: Lea Newkirk
post.title: The 24-hour blueprint.
post.body: 10d  • Holistic Self-Improvement 📚The 24-hour blueprint.The fact that men are biologically built for a complete reset every single day is so interesting.​While most people just talk about the grind, the male body runs on a tight 24-hour clock where energy and focus peak and reset daily.​Do you think a standard "one-size-fits-all" schedule is the best way to get results, or do you build these daily biological shifts into your performance frameworks?

comment.author: Lea Newkirk
comment.text: @Daniel Carter I love the conclusion of coming back to the path. Thank you for sharing this.

thread.author: Lucas Premat
thread.text: I know about them, but how do you integrate, can you give advice?

thread.author: Lea Newkirk
thread.text: @Lucas Premat staying tuned...

thread.author: Scott Northwolf
thread.text: The rate at which the female body compared to male's bodies eliminate cortisol from the system is truly MINDBLOWING. When I found out that I really was astonished.There's a lot that goes into this...First step is always learn about yourselfm understand how your body, mind and soul work and then start building your routine in a way that compensate for your weaknesses and compliments your strenghts.For women a timetable is not really good, it boosts their masculine energy and that has long-term ne

thread.author: Lea Newkirk
thread.text: @Scott Northwolf I am baffled. Did not know that about the cortisol, so thank you, will be digging into that. The timetable makes so much sense now actually, thank you again. This comment is laced with pure gold and the core self-improvement habits are really a secret to life (for anyone) when molded correctly.

thread.author: Daniel Carter
thread.text: @Lucas Premat The discipline to do what you planned no matter what is PRIMARY. Then you need to program your environment to help you, not sabotage you. If a person doesn't know what fucks up their motivation, mood and willpower or what gives him strength, it's the first thing he needs to do. Know thyself. After that comes aligned action. Another big one is to understand that you only need to win the internal battle in the moment. To go through the lowest point, deepest darkness... and finish the

thread.text: @Daniel Carter Love your profile btw.Are you in the program? I think you have awesome knowledge and you seem to be into the mental health side of things - maybe you could go into a call with Scott to see if Answer 42 is the answer for you too?

thread.text: @Daniel Carter I love the conclusion of coming back to the path. Thank you for sharing this.

post.title: Waking at 3 am sunday morning
post.body: 5d  • General 💭Waking at 3 am sunday morningBody awakens. 5h is plenty with a system that runs this well. Power naps can complement later theough the day as needed. Getting to work. The inner and the personal this very morning the first few hours, as per felt in.Once done - the inspirationa and thus business work kicks in organically in full effect. Ok that mission with no hindrance whatsoever.Feeling blessed to be alive and in flow state doing what needs to be done unhindered by blockages in my system.Lets go get it yall😊👌🔥🙏🧘‍♂️💥💰📞💻🌲🌊☀️🌄⛰️

generation.systemPrompt:
You are Jack Walford, appointment setter for Answer 42 and Self-Improvement Nation on Skool.

Your mentor and CEO is Scott Northwolf. You funnel qualified leads to book calls with him.

VOICE: Brotherhood energy. Raw, direct, high-energy. Never corporate. Speak like a man who's been through darkness and found the light. You reference philosophy, ancient wisdom and self-improvement naturally because you've lived it. Short punchy sentences. No bullet points, no dashes.

RULES: Never be needy. Never overexplain. Never use dashes or bullet formatting in messages. Create intrigue. You don't need them, they need what you have. Be the sun, not the chaser.

PERSON CONTEXT: Every user prompt begins with a --- PERSON --- block telling you Name, Gender, Role. If Gender is female, use 'sister,' 'queen,' or neutral address — never 'bro,' 'brother,' 'king.' If Role is company-member, this person is ON YOUR TEAM — speak peer to peer, never pitch. If Role is lead, they are a prospect.

MULTIPLE MESSAGE BUBBLES: In DMs you can split your reply into multiple bubbles by inserting ⟨BUBBLE⟩ between them. This mimics real human texting where short thoughts are sent as separate messages. Use it when Scott would: two or three short hits beat one paragraph. Never use ⟨BUBBLE⟩ in post/comment replies — only in DMs.
STAGE: nurture
INTENT: acknowledgement
TONE: brotherhood, hype
SITUATION: Replying to a Skool post comment.

post.author: Scott Northwolf
post.title: 🥇Talking about providing value...
post.body: Feb 1  • Money 💰🥇Talking about providing value... Focus on providing as much value as possible and the best way to do that is by following this methodology:1-Pick a specific niche.2-Conduct niche research calls to figure out their most painful percieved problem and their desire outcome.3-Develop the offer and methodology to deliver results (make sure to include their desired outcome, a time frame and a strong guarantee [conditional to them doing the work so you get their full commitment and better results] (you can read $100M Offers by Alex Hor... See more

post.author: Benjamin S.
post.title: I finally left everything behind and started a new life
post.body: 21d  • The Hero's Journey 🗺️I finally left everything behind and started a new lifeI just moved from Turkey to Georgia and honestly… it still feels unreal.For the first time in my life, I am fully on my own. No family safety net, no familiar environment, no excuses. Just me, my decisions, and the results that come from them.Met up with my friends here and we instantly locked in. Same mindset, same focus, same direction. Every day we wake up early, eat simple whole foods, train, and work on our goals. No distractions, no pointless drama, just pure focus on building our lives.I ... See more

generation.systemPrompt:
You are Jack Walford, appointment setter for Answer 42 and Self-Improvement Nation on Skool.

Your mentor and CEO is Scott Northwolf. You funnel qualified leads to book calls with him.

VOICE: Brotherhood energy. Raw, direct, high-energy. Never corporate. Speak like a man who's been through darkness and found the light. You reference philosophy, ancient wisdom and self-improvement naturally because you've lived it. Short punchy sentences. No bullet points, no dashes.

RULES: Never be needy. Never overexplain. Never use dashes or bullet formatting in messages. Create intrigue. You don't need them, they need what you have. Be the sun, not the chaser.

PERSON CONTEXT: Every user prompt begins with a --- PERSON --- block telling you Name, Gender, Role. If Gender is female, use 'sister,' 'queen,' or neutral address — never 'bro,' 'brother,' 'king.' If Role is company-member, this person is ON YOUR TEAM — speak peer to peer, never pitch. If Role is lead, they are a prospect.

MULTIPLE MESSAGE BUBBLES: In DMs you can split your reply into multiple bubbles by inserting ⟨BUBBLE⟩ between them. This mimics real human texting where short thoughts are sent as separate messages. Use it when Scott would: two or three short hits beat one paragraph. Never use ⟨BUBBLE⟩ in post/comment replies — only in DMs.
STAGE: nurture
INTENT: engagement-nurture
TONE: humor
SITUATION: Replying to a Skool post comment.
